metadata.goal_text: The student can identify which bar has greater shaded area.

steps.dialogue: Here are three bars with one part shaded in each. Which bar shows the smaller [vocab]fraction[/vocab]?

prompt.text: Select the bar representing a smaller fraction.

step.dialogue: Not quite. Compare the size of the shaded parts in each bar.

events.name: A_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the first or the top bar with equal parts

events.name: B_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the second bar with unequal parts

step.dialogue: Let's look more carefully. [event:A_compare_shaded][event:B_compare_shaded] When we compare the shaded parts, notice how the top bar is divided into more parts, making each piece smaller.

events.name: A_B_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the bars of the first bar with the second bar

step.dialogue: Let me show you how to compare these fractions. [event:restore_snapshot_prompt_start]  [event:A_B_compare_shaded] The top bar shows 1 out of 8 equal parts - that's one-eighth. The middle bar shows 1 out of 4 eq	ual parts - that's one-fourth. The bottom bar shows 1 out of 3 equal parts - that's one-third. When the whole is divided into more parts, each part gets smaller. So [event:A_select] one-eighth is smaller than one-fourth and one-third.

on_correct.dialogue: Correct. [fraction numerator=1 denominator=6] one-sixth [/fraction] is smaller than [fraction numerator=1 denominator=4] one-fourth [/fraction] and smaller than [fraction numerator=1 denominator=3] one-third [/fraction].

metadata.goal_text: The student can identify which bar has greater shaded area.

steps.dialogue: Here are three bars with different amounts shaded. Which one shows a greater [vocab]fraction[/vocab]?

prompt.text: Select the bar with the greater fraction.

step.dialogue: Not quite. Compare how much is shaded in each bar.

events.name: A_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the first or the top bar with equal parts

events.name: B_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the second bar with unequal parts

step.dialogue: Let's look more carefully. Notice how much of the top bar is shaded [event:6920b6b3c5d06478] compared to the bottom bar. Which one has more shaded area?

events.name: A_B_compare_shaded
events.description: Compare the shaded parts of the bars of the first bar with the second bar

step.dialogue: Let me show you how to compare these fractions. [event:restore_snapshot_prompt_start] The top bar [event:6920b727faf32d7e] shows 5 out of 6 parts shaded - that's five-sixths. The bottom bar shows 4 out of 5 parts shaded - that's four-fifths. Five-sixths is greater because it's closer to a whole bar. [event: A_select] The top bar has the greater [vocab]fraction[/vocab].

on_correct.dialogue: Correct. Five-sixths is greater than four-sixths and greater than three-fifths.
